description_long: This is a large, cylindrical metal barrel with a weathered, rusty surface and several visible stains and streaks, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The barrel features prominent yellow radioactive warning symbols on its sides and top, indicating it is used for hazardous or nuclear waste storage. The top surface is flat with several small, round bolt heads or rivets arranged near the edge. One side of the barrel is partially overgrown with green vines, further emphasizing its abandonment or long-term placement outdoors. The surface texture is rough and matte, with visible corrosion and grime. The overall appearance is industrial and utilitarian, with no visible handles or openings aside from the sealed top.
description: A large, rusty metal barrel with radioactive warning symbols, stains, and some overgrown vines, suggesting hazardous waste storage in an outdoor or abandoned setting.
description_view_0: The front view shows a radioactive warning symbol on the side and top, with visible rust, stains, and a mostly clear surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights another radioactive symbol on the side and top, with more pronounced rust streaks and a hint of vegetation on the right edge.
description_view_2: The side view reveals a large patch of green vines growing up the barrel, with the radioactive symbol partially visible on the top.
description_view_3: This view shows a radioactive symbol on the side, rust streaks, and a small section of vines on the left edge.
